variablerow-is-recursive-renders-itself-.description: VariableRow is recursive: renders itself for each child after lazy-loaded expansion; depth controls paddingLeft visually (12 + depth * 16).

view-router-uses-switch-on-activeview.description: View-router uses switch on activeView in renderMainContent / renderChatContent — do NOT add new views via conditional && chains, follow the switch pattern.

visibleids-set-computed-once-when-filter.description: VisibleIds Set computed once when filter is active so chain pruning is O(chains).

vite-worker-import-returns-a-worker.description: Vite ?worker import returns a Worker constructor — `new editorWorker()` spawns an instance.

wave-1-exemption-convention-root-tasks.description: Wave 1 exemption convention — root tasks have no upstream dependencies and may have no downstream dependents (e.g. terminal write tasks).

whitelist-gated-subscription-contextbrid.description: Whitelist-gated subscription: contextBridge exposes 'on' but channel names are validated against PUSH_CHANNELS before registering the listener

workspace-dashboard-linking-has-three-st.description: Workspace↔Dashboard linking has THREE storage layers: (1) ideWorkspaceManager localStorage map, (2) ws.dashboardId in React state, (3) saveDashboardProject mapping. All three must agree.

wrapper-title-is-empty-string-because.description: Wrapper title is empty string ('') because this modal renders its own custom header layout (id + title block) instead of using Modal's default title bar.

write-once-enforcement-via-comparing-exi.description: Write-once enforcement via comparing existing-on-disk to new-content from tool_input

naming.description: Command names are written with leading `!` and placeholders use `{name}` braces for runtime paths or arguments.
Renderer components use .jsx extensions, PascalCase component names, and @ alias imports for src/ui modules.
Guide pages use numeric filename prefixes so the in-app Guide modal can display them in a stable reading order.
Activate via the wrapper div className `log-panel.expanded`; the collapse/expand chevron is ⌄ (▾).
All classes prefixed with .preview-* (BEM-flat style).
All function names use camelCase synapseSomething for any global-leak; locals are short.
Class names mix .task-editor-* with .settings-app-* (rows/labels/inputs reused from SettingsModal grid).
Class names: .claude-* prefix throughout (.claude-conversation, .claude-tool-call, .claude-message-text) — reflects original Claude-SDK styling that has been kept even when adapted for Codex.
Class names: .commands-* prefix throughout.
Class names: .permission-* prefix throughout (.permission-overlay, .permission-modal, .permission-header, .permission-actions).
Class prefix: .debug-console-*.
Class prefix: .debug-panel-* (note: singular 'panel' — distinct from .debug-panels container).
Class prefix: .editor-tab*, .editor-tabs-bar.
Class prefix: .ide-explorer-*.
Class prefix: .ide-search-*.
Class prefix: .problems-panel-*, .problems-summary-*.
Class prefix: .sidebar-* (kebab case).
Class prefixes: .debug-toolbar-*, .problems-panel-*, .debug-console-*, .debug-panels-*.
CSS class 'ide-search-' prefix.
Default export is the component named like the file.
Filename and component are PascalCase: Modal.jsx exports default Modal.
FILTER_LEVELS constant declared at module top; level names are lowercase.
_newWaveName is a temporary marker passed through TaskEditorModal — stripped before persisting.
.jsx entry point named main.jsx (Vite default).
`SYNAPSE_ROOT = path.resolve(__dirname, '../..')` is the canonical tracker_root reference inside electron/services/.
Action types are SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE namespaced: CLAUDE_*, IDE_*, GIT_*, DEBUG_*, DIAGNOSTICS_*, PREVIEW_*, CHAT_TAB_*.
Alias key uses bare `@` (no `@/` slash suffix).
All console.error/warn messages are prefixed with `[watcher]` for log filtering.
All exported functions are named, no class wrapper.
All exports are named — no default export.
All popup overlays share '.sidebar-delete-overlay' / '.sidebar-delete-popup' class skeleton (associate-project, rename, delete) — keep them visually consistent.
All public fns log lifecycle transitions with `[TerminalService]` prefix.
All public functions return either a structured result object `{success, name, filePath, error?}` or a parsedCommand `{name, title, purpose, syntax, content, filePath, lastModified}`.
Approval signal is the literal string 'Approval granted' (case-insensitive) at info level
Archive layout: Archive/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}/
Archive name format: `${YYYY-MM-DD}_${taskName}`.
BEM-like modifiers via dot suffixes (.stat-card.stat-active, .progress-fill.progress-complete).
Boolean flags default to false; numeric timings have ms units in the name (initPollMs, progressRetryMs, reconcileDebounceMs); paths default to null
broadcastFn(eventName, data) is injected by the caller (server/index.js wires SSEManager.broadcast OR Electron pushes IPC) — service is transport-agnostic.
Captures DIR via `$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)` to get an absolute path safely.
Channel names follow kebab-case: 'get-dashboards', 'spawn-worker', 'git-stage', 'ide-read-file'
Char-per-token ratios as named constants (CHAR_RATIOS) at module top.
Class names: .task-details-* prefix shared with AgentDetails (both use .task-details-meta, .task-details-badges, .task-details-prompt — visual consistency).
Class naming uses kebab-case feature prefixes (.header-*, .stat-*, .agent-card-*, .wave-*, .chain-*, .log-*, .timeline-*, .metrics-*, .home-*, .commands-modal-*, .claude-*, .bottom-panel-*).
Class prefix: .ide-view, .workspace-tab-* (this file is the only one defining .workspace-* classes).
Console-log every lifecycle transition prefixed with `[ClaudeCodeService]` for easy log-grepping.
Console-log every lifecycle transition with `[DebugService]` prefix.
CSS class 'ide-explorer-' prefix for all elements.
CSS class 'ide-welcome-' prefix.
CSS class 'problems-' prefix for all elements.
CSS namespace 'chat-' prefix for all chat-dashboard-specific classes (chat-agent-row, chat-wave-column, chat-dashboard-empty, etc.) so they can't collide with the code dashboard's selectors.

Dashboard binding fields are explicit: Synapse_Instance_Location, DashboardID, Synapse_Dashboard, Target_Directory, Context_Directories — these names appear in agent/master and worker docs and must stay stable.
dirBasename helper extracts last path segment for display.
Error format: bullet point per violation prefixed with `\n- `
Event names emitted: 'initialization', 'logs', 'agent_progress', 'tasks_unblocked', 'dashboards_changed', 'queue_changed', 'write_rejected' — the canonical SSE/IPC event vocabulary.
Final message prefixed with emoji '⚠️ PROGRESS LOG QUALITY:'
Functional module, named exports.
Functions that mutate disk are prefixed with underscore (_load, _save) to mark them as internal
Handlers grouped by domain with banner comments (--- Section Name ---)
Hash-prefixed comments (`# Runtime data`, etc.) group related rules.
Hex agent ids are 4-character lowercase produced by createChatAgent IPC; ALWAYS prefixed with 'chat-agent-' before being used as a dashboard key.
History file format: `${YYYY-MM-DD}_${safeTaskName}.json` in HISTORY_DIR.
Hook named useDashboardData (singular) — only ONE caller, do not duplicate.
Hook names are useFooBar (camelCase prefix 'use').
IDE methods are namespaced with 'ide' prefix; git methods with 'git' prefix; debug methods with 'debug' prefix; preview methods with 'preview' prefix
Inline rename input commits on Enter or blur, cancels on Escape; pressing Enter prevents default form submission.
ISO-8601 UTC timestamps with Z suffix and no fractional seconds.
JSX file extension (.jsx) with default export named App.
Lightweight summary shape: { id, task: {name, type, directory, total_tasks, created} | null, agentCount, status } — used directly by QueuePopup.jsx without further transformation.
localStorage keys all prefixed with 'synapse-' to avoid collisions with the host's other localStorage usage.
Message includes emoji prefix and the literal JSON snippet to paste
Method naming uses camelCase even though channel strings are kebab-case (e.g. getDashboards -> 'get-dashboards', spawnWorker -> 'spawn-worker')
Module is functional (no class), exports a flat object of named functions.
Number color via class names (.total/.completed/.in-progress/.failed/.pending/.elapsed); active state via .stat-active modifier.
PROJECT_ROOT computed once via path.resolve(__dirname, '..')
Push channel names use snake_case (initialization, agent_progress, dashboards_changed); pull channels use kebab-case (get-dashboards, save-conversation)
Push event names use snake_case: 'agent_progress', 'dashboards_changed', 'init_state'
Quoted `"$(dirname "$0")"` to handle paths with spaces.
Reducer returns new state via spread; every case immutably copies maps with { ...state.foo, [key]: val }.
Same `[CodexService]` log prefix convention as the Claude service.
Schema validators are pure functions named `isValid<Shape>`.
Section comments use box-drawing characters: // ── SectionName ────
Section CSS uses 'dbg-' prefix (dbg-section, dbg-var-row, dbg-stack-row, dbg-bp-row, dbg-watch-row).
Section markers are uppercase with trailing colon (YOUR PROGRESS FILE:, RETURN FORMAT, PROJECT ROOT:, etc.)
settings-* CSS class prefix throughout.
Snake-shaped name prefixes: `DEFAULT_`, `_DIR`, `_MS`.
Test-only internals exposed under `_` prefix in module.exports.
Three-letter regex/case/word toggle buttons with monospace icon glyphs ('.*', 'Aa', 'ab' underlined).
Title is the bare product name 'Synapse'.
Uses 'synapse-' prefix on all storage keys.

file_organization.description: Agent-control documents use `{tracker_root}`, `{project_root}`, and `{dashboardId}` placeholders instead of machine-specific paths.
Shell examples use fenced `bash` code blocks.
Profiles keep role/priorities/constraints/output-style/success-criteria sections so they can layer onto any command.
Shebang `#!/bin/bash`.
Section banners: /* ----- Title ----- */.
All dialogs use role='dialog' and aria-modal='true' — accessibility baked in.
Class naming: git-manager-dialog-overlay (backdrop) → git-manager-dialog (content with level class) → header/body/footer subdivision.
Default export; FAB-style positioning is in CSS (queue-fab class).
Default export; no internal state.
Default export; uses CSS classes progress-track + progress-fill (+progress-complete).
Default export.
Helper formatters (formatDuration, formatPct) defined at module scope.
Inline SVG (no asset file), strokeWidth 1.5, opacity 0.3 — matches the chat dashboard empty-state styling.
Inline SVG icons (ChevronIcon, FileIcon).
Inline SVG icons (DangerIcon, WarningIcon, SafeIcon) at top of file; LEVEL_ICONS map keys 'danger'|'warning'|'safe'.
JSX comment header at top of file describes purpose in 1-3 lines.
Reuses .modal-message and .modal-btn classes shared with ConfirmModal.
Row keying combines timestamp + index to handle duplicates.
Styles are global class selectors under src/ui/styles or subpage style directories.
Sub-components (CommandFolder, CommandViewer, CommandEditor, CommandGenerator) live in the same file rather than separate modules — convention for tightly-coupled modal-internal pieces.
Sub-components ToolCallBlock, ConversationItem inline in same file.
Two-space indentation.
Two-space JSON indentation.
Var (not let/const) — file uses ES5-compatible syntax for maximum webview compatibility.
.command extension is the macOS Finder convention for double-clickable shell scripts.
'pending-chat-<timestamp>' is the transient pseudo-key for the new-chat flow; never persist this beyond finalize.
Action buttons use class header-action-btn with optional modifier (header-commands-btn).
Action menu items use class 'chat-tab-menu-item'; destructive actions add 'chat-tab-menu-item-danger'.
additions/deletions counters render only when > 0 to keep the header compact.
All directory exports are absolute paths (never relative).
All entry types defined as TYPE_CLASS_MAP / TYPE_LABELS twin maps for symmetry.
All icons styled via CSS vars (--icon-folder, --icon-folder-bg, --icon-generic-bg).
All mutations return the full updated init object so callers can immediately re-render without a separate read.
All path tokens emitted in the prompt use backtick-wrapped absolute paths so the worker's parser/markdown can render them verbatim.
All preview-* CSS classes scoped under .preview-view in preview-view.css.
All public functions take a single `opts` object (taskId, dashboardId, projectDir, prompt, systemPrompt, model, cliPath, dangerouslySkipPermissions, additionalContextDirs, resumeSessionId).
All sync functions swallow filesystem errors and return null/empty defaults — never throw to caller.
All three error/exit code paths converge on delete-from-activeWorkers + broadcast — no leaks even on spawn error.
allow()/block() helper functions for consistent JSON output
Always touches `updated` to new Date().toISOString() on save.
ArchiveEntry uses .home-clickable to imply interactivity; only ArchiveEntry is clickable, ArchiveEntry vs HistoryEntry intentionally diverge.
Arrow buttons toggle replace row (.ide-search-mode-toggle) — chevron rotates via 'expanded' class.
Async fs APIs (fs.promises) for the write path — updateText is async.
backgroundColor: '#0a0a0c' set on BrowserWindow to avoid white flash before renderer paints
block reason includes both assigned and target IDs plus the source for diagnostics
Block reason instructs the agent on the correct action ('Create a worker task instead')
Block reason JSON-escaped to preserve formatting
broadcastFn channels in use: `swarm-state` (state transitions only). Worker output / completion events flow through ClaudeCodeService/CodexService.
Button CSS classes 'debug-toolbar-btn' base + '--restart' / '--stop' modifiers for color theming.
Class naming: .git-manager-{section}-{element}-{state}.
Close 'x' is a ×.
Color values pulled from utils/constants STATUS_COLORS/STATUS_BG_COLORS rather than CSS variables to enable per-status alpha computation via colorWithAlpha.
Console logs use a leading two-space indent (e.g. `  [http] ...`, `  Synapse server listening...`) for visual grouping in terminal output.
Constants DEFAULT_PANEL_HEIGHT, MIN_PANEL_HEIGHT, HEADER_HEIGHT defined at module scope.
Constants MIN_/MAX_/DEFAULT_EXPLORER_WIDTH at top of file.
CSS class 'editor-tab' base + ' active' modifier when active.
CSS class 'workspace-tabs' container, 'workspace-tab' base + ' active' modifier, 'workspace-tab-close' for ×.
CSS imports happen alongside the entry, not inside components.
Dashboard state values: 'running', 'paused', 'replanning', 'cancelled', 'completed'.
dashboardLabel string formatting: 'dashboard1' → 'Dashboard 1' (replace 'dashboard' with 'Dashboard ').
Date stamping uses `new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)` — UTC date, not local.
Decoration arrays are RETURN VALUES of deltaDecorations and immediately reassigned — never push/splice into the existing array.
Default budgetLimit = 8000 across both formatBudget and checkBudget.
Default cols=80, rows=24 — standard terminal geometry.
Default dashboard ID is the literal string 'dashboard1' when none exist.
Default Wave 1 created when first agent is added with no existing waves.
DEFAULT_INITIALIZATION/DEFAULT_LOGS spread into a fresh object on every read so callers can mutate without polluting the constant
Defensive try/catch around every fs.readFileSync — partial results are preferred over hard failures.
Dependency identifiers are task ids (strings).
Each kill prints a confirmation with the PID for traceability.
Each section's JSX is wrapped in CollapsibleSection title + count props.
Echo blank lines before/after status messages for readability in the Terminal popup window.
Electron main-process files use CommonJS require/module.exports rather than ESM imports.
Empty states use git-manager-empty class; line classes are git-manager-diff-line + added|removed|context.
Error messages for 400 responses include the validation rule (`alphanumeric with hyphens, underscores, or dots (max 100 chars, no path traversal)`).
Errors are accumulated per-file in an `errors[]` array rather than throwing.
ES module config (uses `import` rather than `require`).
ES5-style `var` and `function() {}` (anomalous in this codebase).
Exit 0 in all paths.
Exit code 1 on conflict, 0 on success.
FILE_STATUS_COLORS map keys to var(--color-completed)|var(--color-failed)|etc. for the file-status letters in expanded rows.
Helper allow()/block() with JSON output
Helper components defined below the default export inside the same file (AgentRow at the bottom, demo loader as async function).
Hook command paths are relative to project root with `.sh` extension
Hook stores mutable drag state in useRef (never useState) — drag state is not part of render.
Inline style colors use CSS custom properties (var(--color-type-bg) etc.) so theme switching works.
Inline SVG folder icon.
Inline SVG icon components per file type (JsIcon, TypeScriptIcon, CssIcon, JsonIcon, HtmlIcon, MarkdownIcon, GenericFileIcon, FolderIcon, FolderOpenIcon).
Inline SVG icons (CodeBracketIcon, FolderOpenActionIcon, FolderPlusIcon).
Inline SVG icons (GitBranchIcon, FolderOpenIcon) at top of file.
Inline SVG icons (GitBranchTabIcon and inline path elements for + and × in the header).
Inline SVG icons (GitInitIcon, CheckIcon, SpinnerIcon) at top of file.
Inline SVG icons (GitSpinnerIcon, DiffIcon, HistoryIcon, BranchIcon, GitRepoIcon) live at the bottom of the file.
Inline SVG icons (no icon library) with viewBox 16x16 and stroke-width 1.3-1.5.
Inline SVG icons rather than icon font/library.
Inline SVG icons; classes mode-menu-btn / mode-menu-dropdown / mode-menu-item.
Inline-styled chips/labels use var(--text-tertiary), var(--color-blocked), var(--border) tokens for theming consistency.
Inner sub-component ArchiveEntry encapsulates one row; same one-component-per-row pattern as HistoryModal's HistoryEntry and AnalyticsSection.
Inner SVG icon components (ChevronIcon, CheckboxCheckedIcon, AddIcon, RemoveIcon) follow currentColor stroke pattern.
Input fields share 'debug-toolbar-input' class with --script / --args modifiers.
IPC route helpers placed inside main.jsx scope (not exported) — keep this file as the only fetch shim location.
JSON output uses 2-space indent (`JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)`).

Layout CSS classes 'ide-view', 'ide-main', 'ide-explorer-panel', 'ide-divider', 'ide-editor-area', 'ide-editor-content', 'ide-editor-and-debug', 'ide-editor-main', 'ide-debug-sidebar', 'ide-editor-empty'.
MODEL_OPTIONS hard-coded at module scope; resolveModel() falls back to provider's first option if saved value is invalid.
Module-level `let heartbeatTimer = null` is used for the singleton timer reference.
Module-level state (dashboardWatchers Map, dashboardsDirWatcher, queueDirWatcher, lastKnownProgress, three timers) — singleton pattern, not safe to instantiate twice.
Move to New Branch uses an inline custom dialog (NOT ConfirmDialog) because it needs a text input.
No `type: module` — project mixes CommonJS (electron services) and ESM (vite/react via .jsx).
Order within hooks[] = execution order
Output JSON is pretty-printed with 2-space indent (JSON.stringify(summary, null, 2)) — diff-friendly when committed.
OutputLine is a memoizable inner component (not memoized currently — opportunity for perf if buffer grows).
Path construction always via path.join with constants — never string concat.
Per-item action buttons class chain: dashboard-item-action-btn (+chat-action-btn / dashboard-delete-btn).
PlanPreview is an inner sub-component for the modal's preview block.
PORT defaults to 3456 (from constants) but is overridable via `PORT` env var.
Pure functional component, no internal state apart from ElapsedTimer's display string.
Pure module — no I/O, no logging, no side effects (file reads happen inside dependencies).
Push event handlers always check data.dashboardId before dispatching to avoid undefined-keyed updates.
Read-only module, sync + async parallel pairs (matches DashboardService convention).
Refresh helpers `refreshBranches` (branches+currentBranch) and `refreshAll` (also status) are reused by every mutation handler.

Refs end with `Ref` (editorRef, modelRef, currentFilePathRef, fileIdRef, originalContentRef, diagnosticsTimerRef, fileLoadFailedRef, breakpointDecorationsRef, diagnosticDecorationsRef, debugLineDecorationsRef, gutterClickDisposableRef, onChangeDisposableRef).
Relative date helper (relativeDate) covers s/m/h/d/w/mo/y units — used in commit row dates.
Return shape from spawnWorker is intentionally minimal `{pid, taskId, dashboardId}` — full state lives in the activeWorkers map and is fetched via getActiveWorkers().
Returns boolean from delete operation, returns string id from create operation, returns array from list operation — same convention as DashboardService.
Returns either `{success: true, file, line, oldText, newText}` or `{success: false, error}`.
Returns errors as `{error: 'message'}` for projectPath validation; missing-file conditions inside the scan are silently skipped.
Returns numerical summaries (filesScanned/filesModified/labelsAdded/skipped) for !instrument output.
Returns plain JS objects/arrays, no classes.
Same `opts.dangerouslySkipPermissions` boolean used for both providers — the service maps it internally to the provider-appropriate flag.
Same DEFAULT_EXTENSIONS list (.jsx/.tsx/.html/.htm) — likewise.
Section header buttons stop propagation so clicking action buttons doesn't toggle the section collapse.
Section labels (.timeline-section-label) separate Current Task from History; an empty state renders centered when both arrays are empty.
Settings reloaded inline via require('./settings') in some handlers — leverages Node's module cache
Shebang `#!/bin/bash` (despite zsh being macOS default — bash is more portable here).
Shell prompt rendering is the PTY's job — TerminalView never injects prompt strings.
Sidebar buttons use `sidebar-tab` + `.active` modifier; tab rows are .sidebar-tab-row inside .sidebar-tab-bar.
Single side-effect module imported once before Monaco usage.
Single source of truth — no other file should redeclare these values.
Skip dotfile dirs and node_modules/dist when scanning.
Snapshots always re-read app.getVersion() so the version field cannot drift.
spawnTerminal returns minimal info, full state lives in activeTerminals (fetched via getActiveTerminals).
splitFilePath uses lastIndexOf('/') — assumes POSIX-style paths in progress JSON, which is consistent with how workers report file changes.
Status is derived from `task.total_tasks > 0 && progressValues.length < totalTasks` — a swarm with declared tasks is `in_progress` until all progress files exist.
Status-color hex codes hardcoded (#34d399, #9b7cf0, #6E6E73) — these duplicate STATUS_COLORS in constants.js and are NOT theme-synced.
StatusBadge is a sibling export rather than a default to allow direct import in pipeline headers.
Storage key 'synapse-ide-workspace-dashboards' (plural).
Sub-section banners: /* ----- Title ----- */
Surface-aware dispatch actions: CLAUDE_APPEND_MSG / CLAUDE_STASH_APPEND_MSG / CLAUDE_TAB_STASH_APPEND_MSG / CLAUDE_UPDATE_MESSAGES all carry `surface`.
Switch from 'Hh Mm' to 'Mm Ss' format triggered when hours > 0 or minutes > 0 respectively.
Tab IDs: 'default' for the first tab; new tabs get string ids assigned by the reducer.
Task IDs are strings shaped 'W.N' (wave.subtask) — must be unique across the dashboard.
Terminal IDs (1, 2, ...) are local per-dashboard, not global. Tab labels follow `Terminal {id}`.
TERMINAL_OPTIONS at module scope; theme function (getTerminalThemeFromCSS) is called both at definition time and on mutation.
Themes are entire :root blocks gated by [data-theme=...] attribute on root element.
Time inputs are ISO strings; outputs are display strings.
Title bar formatting matches `Start Synapse.command` for visual consistency.
Title bars use `=` characters and centered spacing.
titleBarStyle: 'hiddenInset' on darwin only, 'default' elsewhere
TMP_MANIFEST=${MANIFEST}.tmp.$$ uses PID for collision safety
Trailing `read -n 1` is required because Terminal.app closes the window when a .command exits.
Trailing newline omitted in this file.
Trailing slashes denote directories.
type field is always `command` (no other type used)
useCallback for cleanupDrag so it can be referenced from both pointerup and effect teardown without creating closures with stale refs.
Uses chain.id and wave.id as React keys; chain.tasks is an array of task ids (strings).
Uses ES5 'var' throughout (no let/const) — leftover from extraction from legacy dashboard.js per file header.
useState for purely-local UI flags (timelineOpen, selectedAgent, showConfirm); global state goes through dispatch.
Versions use caret `^` ranges (allow minor and patch updates).
Wave column class chain: wave-column (+wave-active / +wave-done) mirrors wave-header modifiers.
Wave header shows count badge only when totalCount > 0.
Wave is a 1-indexed integer (or numeric string).
Wildcard `*` follows directory paths (e.g., `dashboards/*`).
Worker labels match Monaco's internal language IDs.
Wrapper div uses class 'chat-instance-claude-wrap' so chat-mode CSS can override ClaudeView's default layout without touching the shared component.

api.description: All git API calls go through window.electronAPI.git* and check `result.success` before reading `result.data`. Errors surface via setOperationError state.
1-based lines/columns are the contract on the public API; 0-based only inside CDP boundaries.
All git API calls use window.electronAPI.git* and check `result?.success` before reading `result.data`.
All IPC calls guarded by `window.electronAPI?.method` optional chaining.
All IPC results checked for `result.success` before reading `result.data`; errors surface to showResult which formats them by type.
All public functions return `{success, error?}` for action endpoints.
Conversation IDs: 'conv_' + Date.now(); stored to disk via api.saveConversation.
Each handle*Confirm function follows the pattern: setLoading(true) → setError(null) → try/catch IPC call → refreshGitData → setActiveDialog(null) → showSuccess(...).
Each ipcMain.handle uses async () => and returns a plain object (success/data/error envelope for git+ide; raw shape for dashboard reads)
Plan schema mirrors the spec in agent/master/initialization_blueprint.md verbatim
Planning prompt is a multi-line concatenation in buildPlanningPrompt — JSON schema is inlined verbatim.
Public API: single renderMarkdown(text) — keep additional features internal.
Replan output schema is fixed: {summary, modified[], added[], removed[], retry[]} — see SwarmOrchestrator.applyReplan for the consumer.
Sync fs APIs throughout — invoked at IPC time, never in hot loops.
Synchronous fs API — runs once at command time, blocking is acceptable.
Synchronous fs APIs throughout — these run on the Electron main process at IPC time, not in render frames.
Tasks transformed via taskToAgent() before passing to AgentCard — keeps AgentCard contract uniform between code and chat dashboards.

error_handling.description: Color tokens via CSS variables (--color-completed, --color-failed, --color-blocked, --color-accent, --color-type, --color-duration) rather than constants.js — chosen because all values flow through the theme system.
Metric card subcomponent (MetricCard) accepts label/value/sublabel/warn props; warn toggles a CSS modifier.
Modifier classes: .preview-edit-toast--success / --error (BEM double-dash).
All async functions return `{success, ...}` or `{success: false, error}` — consistent with other services.
All checks emit '• ...' bullet items into a WARNINGS string
All public functions wrap their body in try/catch with safe-default returns.
All readers return null on any failure (never throw).
All writers throw on failure (never silent).
AppContext actions: PREVIEW_SET_URL, PREVIEW_SET_LOADING, PREVIEW_SET_ERROR, PREVIEW_ADD_EDIT, PREVIEW_SET_LABEL_MAP.
Async variants only catch readdir/stat failures (`catch { /* dir may not exist */ }`) and return empty defaults.
Console.error for unexpected failures, no UI error state.
Defensive try/catch returning [] / {} from each function — never throws.
Error display via git-manager-error class with git-manager-error-message child.
Error messages prompt for keypress (`read -n 1`) so the Terminal window stays open for the user to read.
Errors and warnings carry both `type` (machine-readable) and `message` (human-readable).
Errors are caught at every async boundary — checkForUpdates().catch(() => {}) on the timer guarantees no unhandled-rejection at startup.
Functions return either a data object or `{error: message}` — never throw.
Human-readable `message` field is set alongside semantic state (checking/available/downloaded/error) for direct UI consumption.
Inline styles dominate for the result/warning/counter elements rather than CSS classes — keeps everything self-contained but makes themeing harder.
Inline SVG icons (IconBranch, IconChevron, IconPlus, IconTrash, IconMerge, IconSwitch, IconMove, IconWarning, IconCheck, IconCloud, IconSearch, IconStar, IconRemote) — no external icon library.
Inline SVG icons (PushIcon, PullIcon, FetchIcon, ForceIcon, ArrowUpIcon, ArrowDownIcon, CheckIcon, BranchSmallIcon, plus DangerDialogIcon, WarningDialogIcon, SafeDialogIcon for the inline dialogs).

Inline SVG icons (SaveIcon, MoveBranchIcon, UndoIcon, DiscardIcon, PullIcon, PushIcon, StashIcon, StashPopIcon, FetchIcon, SyncIcon, plus small ChevronIcon, BranchSmallIcon, CloseIcon, SuccessCheckIcon, ErrorXIcon) — large icons for cards, small icons for chips/buttons.
Log level keys: info, warn, error, debug, permission, deviation.
Push button color flips: warning class on protected branches, success class otherwise — visual cue before the user even clicks.
set -o pipefail for safe error propagation
Severity colors via CSS vars (var(--color-failed,#FF6B6B), var(--color-warning,#FFD93D), var(--color-in-progress,#9B7CF0)).
Status keys: completed, in_progress, pending, failed, claimed, blocked.
task fallback chain: `(init && init.task) ? init.task : {}` — defensive against malformed init.
Try/catch around localStorage on read; quota errors swallowed on write.
Uses showResult abstraction with type 'success'|'error' that maps to var(--color-completed)|var(--color-failed).
var(--token, fallback) pattern for resilience.
warn() emits {message: ...} JSON
warn() emits {message: ...}; ok() exits silently

testing.description: Same SKIP_DIRS list as InstrumentService (node_modules, .next, dist, build, .git) — must stay in sync with InstrumentService and PreviewTextWriter.

The IDE_DEFAULT_IGNORE list ['.git', 'node_modules', '.DS_Store', '__pycache__', '.next', '.cache', 'dist', 'build', '.venv', 'venv'] is the single source of truth for both ide-read-dir and ide-search

documentation.description: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
ASCII double hyphen is used in prose where many docs avoid Unicode punctuation for terminal readability.
JSDoc block above the component describes props.
Comments explain UX (right-click → Open) for non-developer users.
DEFAULTS object groups keys by section comments (Window / Behavior / Performance / Theme / Dashboard metadata / Project)
Header comment 'Mirrors SettingsModal.js' — JSX rewrite of vanilla version.
JSDoc on every exported function with @param/@returns annotations.
Mac dock icon prefers 512x512 PNG over .icns (PNGs are more reliable in Electron's nativeImage)
No `engines` field — Node version is stated only in README.md (18+).
Public function JSDoc comments describe both behavior and parameter shape.
Section comments use `/* ── Title ─── */` divider style.
